title: quercus aliena f. alticupuliformis
intro: Quercus aliena f. alticupuliformis is a variant of the Quercus aliena, commonly known as the Japanese evergreen oak, which is a species of oak tree native to East Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high tannin content, which has been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Quercus aliena f. alticupuliformis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to support digestive health and boost the immune system.
